concept_name: Field of a Charged Spherical Shell: E=0 inside, kq/r² outside
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics, Ch.1 Electric Charges and Fields (field of a uniformly charged spherical shell, §1.15) — table-of-contents + Indian-anchor reference only; teaching authored from first principles. HC Verma consulted for SEQUENCE (we already KNOW Gauss's law Φ = q_enc/ε₀, but that is total flux, not the field at a point → by spherical symmetry E must be radial and have the same magnitude everywhere on a concentric sphere, which is exactly what lets E come out of the flux integral → draw a Gaussian sphere OUTSIDE the shell, r ≥ R: Φ = E·4πr² = q/ε₀ ⇒ E = q/(4πε₀r²) = kq/r² → so from outside the shell looks identical to a point charge q sitting at its centre (PRIMARY aha) → now shrink the Gaussian sphere INSIDE, r < R: it encloses no charge, q_enc = 0 ⇒ Φ = 0 ⇒ E = 0 everywhere inside — not small, exactly zero (SUPPORTING aha) → assemble the full E-vs-r picture: flat zero for r<R, a jump to kq/R² at r=R, then a 1/r² falloff → finally let the student drag the Gaussian-sphere radius across R and watch the arrows vanish inside and follow 1/r² outside), never copied. The atomic idea: applying Gauss's law plus spherical symmetry to a uniformly charged spherical SHELL gives a piecewise field — exactly zero everywhere inside (r<R, q_enc=0) and exactly the point-charge field kq/r² = q/(4πε₀r²) outside (r≥R, the full charge enclosed). The external field depends only on q and r (the distance from the CENTRE), never on the shell radius R; the field jumps discontinuously from 0 to kq/R² across the surface. SCOPE: the charged SHELL only — does NOT re-teach the Gauss statement Φ = q_enc/ε₀ (gauss_law), the open-surface flux definition Φ = E·A (electric_flux), or the solid-sphere / line / plane cases (deferred to future siblings). NEW field_3d gauss_law_sphere scenario (architect FLAG #R1): a concentric charged shell at radius R, an expandable concentric Gaussian sphere of adjustable r (inside or outside), radial E-arrows that vanish inside and follow 1/r² outside, an E-magnitude readout switching regime at r=R (0 inside, kq/r² outside), an E-vs-r plot for STATE_6, and a STATE_7 r slider with stable explorer ID gauss_sphere_explorer (Rule 27). Prerequisites gauss_law + electric_flux. Conceptual-only (no mode_overrides), EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; misconceptions confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a).

real_world_anchor.primary: In the school physics lab, the metal dome of a Van de Graaff generator holds a fixed charge spread over its surface. Stand outside it and the dome pulls or pushes a small charge exactly as if all its charge were squeezed into a single point at its centre — a clean point-charge field. But climb inside that hollow dome and the field drops to nothing: a charge placed inside feels no electric force at all. The same metal shell that acts like a point from outside is a perfect shelter on the inside.
real_world_anchor.secondary: A hollow charged metal ball shields whatever sits inside it — why a car's metal body keeps you safe in a lightning strike and why sensitive instruments are wrapped in metal cans. The closed shell forces the interior field to exactly zero, while outside it behaves as if every bit of charge sat at the centre.

q.name: Total charge on the spherical shell

r_gauss.name: Radius of the Gaussian sphere / field point (distance from centre)

epsilon_0.name: Permittivity of free space (a fixed constant of nature)

DEMO_E_PER_NC.name: Demo field readout per nanocoulomb, at unit demo radius

E_real.formula: ((r_gauss < R) ? 0 : q * 1e-9) / (4 * PI * epsilon_0 * r_gauss * r_gauss)

constraints: E is exactly zero everywhere inside the shell (r < R) - not small, not approximate; q_enc = 0 there (C1).
Outside the shell (r >= R) the field is exactly that of a point charge q at the centre: E = kq/r^2 = q/(4*pi*epsilon_0*r^2) (C2).
E is discontinuous at the shell surface r = R: it jumps from 0 (just inside) to the peak kq/R^2 (just outside) - a step, not a smooth rise (C3).
E is strictly radial everywhere (E parallel r-hat) and has the same magnitude at every point of a concentric sphere - this is what lets E leave the flux integral (C4).
The external field depends only on q and r, NOT on the shell radius R: for fixed r > R, changing R leaves E unchanged (C5).
q_enc(r) is a step function: 0 for r < R, full q for r >= R; the regime switches exactly at r = R (C6).
The flux Phi = E*4*pi*r^2 = q_enc/epsilon_0 is independent of r outside the shell (the 1/r^2 cancels the r^2) - verified numerically (C7).

aha_moment.statement: From outside, a charged shell's field is exactly a point charge kq/r² at its centre.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: The shell's external radial arrows cross-fade into the identical field of a single point charge sitting at the centre — same kq/r² everywhere.

STATE_1.title: We know Φ = q/ε₀ — but what is E(r) inside and outside?

scene_composition.text: Φ = q/ε₀ gives total flux, not E at a point
so what is E(r) inside and outside?
a charged metal shell, radius R

tts_sentences.text_en: Here is a hollow metal ball with charge spread over its surface — like the dome of a Van de Graaff generator.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह देखो — एक hollow metal ball जिसकी surface पर charge फैला हुआ है — जैसे Van de Graaff generator का dome।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది చూడండి — ఒక hollow metal ball, దీని surface మీద charge వ్యాపించి ఉంది — Van de Graaff generator యొక్క dome లాగా।

tts_sentences.text_en: Gauss's law already tells us the total flux out of any closed surface is q over epsilon-nought. But that is the total, not the field at one point.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Gauss's law हमें पहले ही बता देता है कि किसी भी closed surface से निकलने वाला total flux है q over epsilon-nought। लेकिन यह total है, किसी एक point पर field नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Gauss's law మనకు ముందే చెప్తుంది — ఏదైనా closed surface నుండి వెలువడే total flux అనేది q over epsilon-nought. కానీ ఇది total flux మాత్రమే, ఒక point వద్ద field కాదు।

tts_sentences.text_en: So here is the real question: what is the electric field E at a distance r — inside the shell, and outside it?
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो असली सवाल यह है: एक distance r पर electric field E क्या होगा — shell के inside, और outside?
tts_sentences.text_te: కాబట్టి అసలు ప్రశ్న ఏమిటంటే: distance r వద్ద electric field E ఎంత ఉంటుంది — shell యొక్క inside లో, మరియు outside లో?

misconception_watch.belief: Knowing the total flux through a surface already tells you the field at every point on it.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The same flux q/ε₀ pierces a sphere whose arrows are all equal — only because symmetry forces E to be the same everywhere can we pull E out and solve for it.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Total flux is not the point field; you need symmetry to turn Φ = q/ε₀ into E(r).

STATE_2.title: By symmetry, E is radial and the same on the whole sphere

scene_composition.text: field point P at radius r
sideways, or straight out?
E ∥ r̂,  E = constant on the sphere

tts_sentences.text_en: Pick a point P at distance r from the centre. In which direction must the field point there?
tts_sentences.text_hi: Centre से r दूरी पर एक point P लो। वहाँ field किस direction में होगी?
tts_sentences.text_te: Centre నుండి r దూరంలో ఒక point P తీసుకోండి। అక్కడ field ఏ direction లో ఉండాలి?

tts_sentences.text_en: Could E point sideways along the sphere, or must it point straight out along the radius?
tts_sentences.text_hi: क्या E sphere के साथ sideways point कर सकता है, या उसे radius के along straight out point करना ही होगा?
tts_sentences.text_te: E sphere వెంబడి sideways point చేయగలదా, లేదా radius వెంబడి straight out point చేయాలా?

tts_sentences.text_en: The shell looks identical from every direction, so E has nothing to pick a sideways direction. It must point radially.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Shell हर direction से बिल्कुल एक जैसी दिखती है, इसलिए E के पास sideways direction चुनने का कोई कारण नहीं। इसे radially point करना ही पड़ेगा।
tts_sentences.text_te: Shell అన్ని directions నుండి సరిగ్గా ఒకే విధంగా కనిపిస్తుంది, కాబట్టి E sideways direction ఎంచుకోవడానికి ఎలాంటి కారణం లేదు। అది radially point చేయాలి।

tts_sentences.text_en: And by that same symmetry its magnitude is the same at every point on a sphere of radius r. That is what lets E come out of the flux integral.
tts_sentences.text_hi: और उसी symmetry की वजह से radius r की sphere पर हर point पर E का magnitude एक समान होता है। इसी कारण E flux integral से बाहर आ सकता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మరియు ఆ అదే symmetry వల్ల, radius r యొక్క sphere మీద ప్రతి point వద్ద E యొక్క magnitude ఒకే విధంగా ఉంటుంది। అందుకే E ని flux integral నుండి బయటికి తీయగలం।

misconception_watch.belief: The field could lean sideways or be stronger on one side of the shell than another.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Rotating the whole picture leaves it unchanged, so the radial arrow at P must have the same length all around — E is purely radial and constant on the sphere.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Spherical symmetry forces E to be radial and equal in magnitude everywhere at radius r.

STATE_3.title: Outside (r ≥ R): Φ = E·4πr² = q/ε₀ ⇒ E = kq/r²

scene_composition.text: Gaussian sphere outside the shell, r ≥ R
Φ = E·4πr² = q/ε₀
E = q/(4πε₀r²) = kq/r²

tts_sentences.text_en: Draw a Gaussian sphere outside the shell, at radius r bigger than R. It encloses the full charge q.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Shell के बाहर एक Gaussian sphere खींचो, radius r पर जहाँ r, R से बड़ा है। यह पूरा charge q को enclose करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Shell బయట ఒక Gaussian sphere గీయండి, radius r వద్ద — r, R కంటే పెద్దది। ఇది పూర్తి charge q ని enclose చేస్తుంది।

tts_sentences.text_en: Now write the flux through it. What is Φ in terms of E and the sphere's area?
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब इसके through flux लिखो। E और sphere की area के terms में Φ क्या होगा?
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు దాని గుండా flux రాయండి। E మరియు sphere యొక్క area పరంగా Φ ఏమవుతుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: Since E is constant and radial, the flux is just E times the area: Φ equals E times four pi r squared.
tts_sentences.text_hi: चूँकि E constant और radial है, flux बस E times area है: Φ equals E times four pi r squared।
tts_sentences.text_te: E constant మరియు radial అయినందువల్ల, flux కేవలం E times area: Φ equals E times four pi r squared.

tts_sentences.text_en: Set that equal to q over epsilon-nought and solve. E equals q over four pi epsilon-nought r squared — that is exactly kq over r squared.
tts_sentences.text_hi: उसे q over epsilon-nought के बराबर रखो और solve करो। E equals q over four pi epsilon-nought r squared — यह exactly kq over r squared है।
tts_sentences.text_te: దాన్ని q over epsilon-nought కి equal చేసి solve చేయండి। E equals q over four pi epsilon-nought r squared — అది exactly kq over r squared.

misconception_watch.belief: You have to integrate the field over the surface piece by piece to get E.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Because E is constant on the Gaussian sphere, the integral collapses to E times 4πr², and one line of algebra gives E = kq/r².
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Constant radial E turns ∮E·dA into E·4πr², so Φ = q/ε₀ gives E = kq/r² directly.

STATE_4.title: From outside, the shell is exactly a point charge at the centre

scene_composition.text: outside field: E = kq/r²
r measured from the centre
outside ≡ point charge q at the centre

tts_sentences.text_en: On the right, a single point charge q.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दाईं तरफ, एक single point charge q।
tts_sentences.text_te: కుడి వైపు, ఒక single point charge q ఉంది।

tts_sentences.text_en: At a distance r, its field is E equals kq over r squared — straight out, weakening as one over r squared.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक distance r पर, इसकी field है E equals kq over r squared — सीधा बाहर, one over r squared के रूप में कमज़ोर होती हुई।
tts_sentences.text_te: distance r వద్ద, దాని field అనేది E equals kq over r squared — నేరుగా బయటికి, one over r squared గా బలహీనమవుతూ।

tts_sentences.text_en: Now think — will a charged shell of the same total charge q give the same field out here, or a different one?
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब सोचो — क्या same total charge q वाला एक charged shell यहाँ बाहर same field देगा, या कोई अलग field?
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు ఆలోచించండి — same total charge q ఉన్న ఒక charged shell ఇక్కడ same field ఇస్తుందా, లేదా వేరే field ఇస్తుందా?

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the left. A charged shell fades in. Draw a Gaussian sphere of radius r around it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: बाईं तरफ देखो। एक charged shell fade in होती है। उसके चारों तरफ radius r की एक Gaussian sphere खींचो।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఎడమ వైపు చూడండి। ఒక charged shell fade in అవుతుంది। దాని చుట్టూ radius r యొక్క ఒక Gaussian sphere గీయండి।

tts_sentences.text_en: Gauss's law gives Φ equals E times four-pi-r-squared equals q over epsilon-nought, so E equals kq over r squared.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Gauss's law देता है Φ equals E times four pi r squared equals q over epsilon-nought, इसलिए E equals kq over r squared।
tts_sentences.text_te: Gauss's law ఇస్తుంది — Φ equals E times four pi r squared equals q over epsilon-nought, కాబట్టి E equals kq over r squared.

tts_sentences.text_en: Exactly the same as the point charge. From outside, the shell is a point charge at its centre — the two formulas below match.
tts_sentences.text_hi: बिल्कुल point charge जैसा। Outside से देखें तो shell अपने centre पर एक point charge है — नीचे के दोनों formulas मेल खाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Point charge తో సరిగ్గా ఒకే విధంగా ఉంది। Outside నుండి చూస్తే, shell తన centre వద్ద ఒక point charge లాంటిది — నిచటి రెండు formulas సరిపోతాయి।

misconception_watch.belief: A bigger shell, or a thicker shell, must give a stronger or different field outside.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The external arrows are identical to those of a point charge; the formula kq/r² has no R and no thickness term, so changing the shell size leaves the outside field unchanged.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Outside, E = kq/r² depends only on q and r from the centre — never on the shell's radius or thickness.

STATE_5.title: Inside (r < R): q_enc = 0 ⇒ Φ = 0 ⇒ E = 0 everywhere

scene_composition.text: Gaussian sphere inside the shell, r < R
q_enc = 0  ⇒  Φ = 0
E = 0  (everywhere inside)

tts_sentences.text_en: Now shrink the Gaussian sphere until it sits entirely inside the shell, at a radius r smaller than R.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब Gaussian sphere को तब तक छोटा करो जब तक वह पूरी तरह shell के inside न आ जाए, एक radius r पर जो R से छोटी हो।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు Gaussian sphere ని అది పూర్తిగా shell యొక్క inside లో వచ్చే వరకు చిన్నది చేయండి, R కంటే చిన్న radius r వద్ద।

tts_sentences.text_en: All the charge lives on the shell, outside this little sphere. So how much charge does it enclose?
tts_sentences.text_hi: सारा charge shell पर है, इस छोटी sphere के outside। तो यह कितना charge enclose करती है?
tts_sentences.text_te: అన్ని charge shell మీద ఉంది, ఈ చిన్న sphere యొక్క outside లో। కాబట్టి ఇది ఎంత charge enclose చేస్తుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: None at all. q enclosed is zero, so the flux through it is zero, and by symmetry E times four pi r squared is zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: बिल्कुल नहीं। q enclosed zero है, तो इसके through flux zero है, और symmetry से E times four pi r squared zero है।
tts_sentences.text_te: అస్సలు ఏమీ లేదు। q enclosed zero, కాబట్టి దాని గుండా flux zero, మరియు symmetry వల్ల E times four pi r squared zero అవుతుంది।

tts_sentences.text_en: That forces E to zero at every interior point — not small, exactly zero. Inside a charged shell the field vanishes completely.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह E को हर interior point पर zero करने पर मजबूर करता है — थोड़ा नहीं, बिल्कुल zero। एक charged shell के inside field पूरी तरह vanish हो जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది ప్రతి interior point వద్ద E ని zero కి బలవంతం చేస్తుంది — కొంచెం కాదు, సరిగ్గా zero. ఒక charged shell లోపల field పూర్తిగా vanish అవుతుంది।

misconception_watch.belief: Inside the shell the field still points outward, just weaker — or it is strongest at the very centre.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: As the Gaussian sphere shrinks all the way to the centre, the arrows collapse to exactly zero everywhere inside; nothing grows toward the middle.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Inside the shell E is exactly zero everywhere because q_enc = 0 — not weak, not centre-peaked, zero.

STATE_6.title: The full picture: flat zero, a jump at R, then 1/r²

scene_composition.text: r < R:  E = 0  (flat)
jump to peak kq/R² at r = R
r ≥ R:  E = kq/r²  (1/r² falloff)

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the Gaussian sphere and the graph move together. Right now the sphere sits inside the shell, and the field is flat at zero — the dot rides along the bottom.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Gaussian sphere और graph को एक साथ move होते देखो। अभी sphere, shell के inside है, और field zero पर flat है — dot नीचे के along चल रहा है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Gaussian sphere మరియు graph కలిసి move అవడం చూడండి। ఇప్పుడు sphere, shell యొక్క inside లో ఉంది, మరియు field zero వద్ద flat గా ఉంది — dot అడుగు వెంట పయనిస్తోంది।

tts_sentences.text_en: Now the sphere grows out to the surface. Right at r equals R the field jumps straight up to its peak value kq over R squared — a sudden step, not a smooth climb.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब sphere surface तक बड़ी होती है। r equals R पर field सीधे ऊपर अपनी peak value kq over R squared तक jump करती है — एक sudden step, smooth climb नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు sphere surface వరకు పెరుగుతుంది। r equals R వద్ద field నేరుగా పైకి peak value kq over R squared వరకు jump చేస్తుంది — ఒక sudden step, smooth climb కాదు।

tts_sentences.text_en: Keep going outside: as r grows past R the arrows shrink and the dot slides down the tail, falling off as one over r squared — the point-charge curve.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Outside जाते रहो: जैसे-जैसे r, R से आगे बढ़ता है, arrows सिकुड़ते हैं और dot tail के नीचे slide करता है, one over r squared की तरह fall off करते हुए — यह point-charge curve है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Outside వైపు కొనసాగండి: r, R ని దాటి పెరిగే కొద్దీ arrows తగ్గిపోతాయి మరియు dot tail వెంట కిందికి slide అవుతుంది, one over r squared లా fall off అవుతూ — ఇది point-charge curve.

tts_sentences.text_en: Zero, a sharp jump, then a one-over-r-squared tail. That jump at the surface is the signature of the shell.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Zero, एक sharp jump, फिर one-over-r-squared tail। Surface पर वह jump shell की पहचान है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Zero, ఒక sharp jump, తర్వాత one-over-r-squared tail. Surface వద్ద ఆ jump అనేది shell యొక్క signature.

misconception_watch.belief: The field rises smoothly as you approach the shell from outside, peaking somewhere and then easing in.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The curve climbs as 1/r² toward the surface, hits its peak kq/R² exactly at r=R, then drops vertically to zero the instant you step inside.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: E is discontinuous at r=R: peak kq/R² just outside, exactly 0 just inside — a step, not a smooth rise.

STATE_7.title: Explore: grab the field point and move it anywhere

scene_composition.text: grab the point P and move it anywhere
live E: 0 inside, kq/r² outside
the regime flips exactly at r = R

tts_sentences.text_en: Now you drive it. Grab the field point P and move it anywhere — the field arrow always points straight out along the radius.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब तुम control करो। field point P को पकड़ो और कहीं भी move करो — field arrow हमेशा radius के along straight out point करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీరు control చేయండి। field point P ని పట్టుకుని ఎక్కడైనా move చేయండి — field arrow ఎప్పుడూ radius వెంబడి straight out point చేస్తుంది।

tts_sentences.text_en: Move it inside the shell and the arrow dies to zero. Move it outward and the arrow shrinks as one over r squared — the same length all around the circle.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसे shell के inside ले जाओ और arrow zero हो जाता है। इसे outward ले जाओ और arrow one over r squared की तरह सिकुड़ता है — circle के चारों तरफ same length।
tts_sentences.text_te: దాన్ని shell యొక్క inside కి move చేయండి, arrow zero అవుతుంది। దాన్ని outward move చేయండి, arrow one over r squared లా తగ్గుతుంది — circle చుట్టూ same length.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the live readout switch regime exactly at the surface. Inside is always zero; outside is always the point-charge field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Live readout को exactly surface पर regime switch करते देखो। Inside हमेशा zero है; outside हमेशा point-charge field है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Live readout ని exactly surface వద్ద regime switch చేయడం చూడండి। Inside ఎప్పుడూ zero; outside ఎప్పుడూ point-charge field.

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered the field of a uniformly charged spherical shell when they can (1) state that the field is exactly zero everywhere inside the shell (r<R) because the enclosed charge is zero there; (2) state that outside the shell (r≥R) the field is exactly that of a point charge q at the centre, E = kq/r² = q/(4πε₀r²); (3) explain that this follows from Gauss's law plus spherical symmetry, which makes E radial and constant on a concentric sphere so that Φ = E·4πr² = q_enc/ε₀; (4) measure r from the CENTRE of the shell, not from its surface, and recognise that the external field does not depend on the shell radius R; and (5) describe the full E-vs-r profile as flat zero inside, a discontinuous jump to the peak kq/R² at r=R, then a 1/r² falloff outside.

questions.stem: A thin uniformly charged spherical shell of radius R carries total charge q. What is the electric field at a point a distance r > R from the centre?

options.A: kq/r² — the same as a point charge q located at the centre
options.B: kq/R² — fixed at the surface value for all points outside
options.C: kq/(r−R)² — measured from the shell's surface, not its centre
options.D: Zero — the charge is on the surface, so there is no field outside

distractor_misconceptions.B: freezes the field at its surface value instead of letting it fall off as 1/r²
distractor_misconceptions.C: measures r from the surface rather than from the centre
distractor_misconceptions.D: confuses the inside (zero) result with the outside region

questions.tested_idea: Outside a charged shell the field equals the point-charge field kq/r² with r from the centre.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A Van de Graaff dome of radius R carries charge q. A small test charge is held at distance r > R from the dome's centre. Write the field it feels in terms of q and r.

questions.stem: For the same charged shell of radius R and charge q, what is the electric field at a point inside the shell, at radius r < R?

options.A: Exactly zero — the Gaussian sphere there encloses no charge
options.B: kq/r² — the field keeps growing as you move toward the centre
options.C: kq/R² — it holds the surface value all the way inside
options.D: A small but nonzero outward field that fades toward the centre

distractor_misconceptions.B: thinks the field is strongest at the centre and rises inward
distractor_misconceptions.C: carries the surface value into the interior instead of zero
distractor_misconceptions.D: believes the inside field is small but not exactly zero

questions.tested_idea: Inside a charged shell the field is exactly zero because q_enc = 0.
questions.parallel_form_stem: You stand inside the hollow metal dome of a Van de Graaff generator. What electric field does a charge placed anywhere inside feel, and why?

questions.stem: Why does the field outside the shell come out to E = kq/r² in just one line of algebra, instead of needing a hard surface integral?

options.A: Spherical symmetry makes E radial and constant on a concentric sphere, so the flux is simply E·4πr² = q/ε₀
options.B: Because the shell's charge is small enough to ignore the integral
options.C: Because flux is always equal to the field times the shell's radius R
options.D: Because the field outside is uniform, so the integral is just E times the area

distractor_misconceptions.B: invokes a fictitious 'small charge' approximation rather than symmetry
distractor_misconceptions.C: uses the shell radius R instead of the Gaussian-sphere radius r in the area
distractor_misconceptions.D: claims the external field is uniform, when it actually varies as 1/r²

questions.tested_idea: Symmetry makes E constant and radial, collapsing ∮E·dA to E·4πr² = q_enc/ε₀.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Explain, using the symmetry of the Van de Graaff dome, why the flux through a sphere drawn around it is just E·4πr² and not a complicated integral.

questions.stem: Two charged shells carry the same charge q but have different radii: shell A has radius R and shell B has radius 2R. Compare the field each produces at the same external point a distance 3R from the centre.

options.A: Identical — outside, E = kq/r² depends only on q and r, never on the shell radius
options.B: The smaller shell A gives a stronger field, since its charge is more concentrated
options.C: The larger shell B gives a stronger field, since it has more surface area
options.D: They cannot be compared without knowing the shell thickness

distractor_misconceptions.B: thinks a smaller shell concentrates the charge into a stronger external field
distractor_misconceptions.C: thinks a larger surface area produces a stronger external field
distractor_misconceptions.D: believes shell thickness enters the external field formula

questions.tested_idea: The external field depends only on q and r from the centre, not on the shell radius R.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A small charged ball and a large charged dome carry the same charge. At the same distance from each centre (both outside), which one's field is stronger?

questions.stem: A shell of radius R carries charge q. What happens to the field as you cross the surface, moving from just inside to just outside?

options.A: It jumps discontinuously from 0 just inside to the peak kq/R² just outside
options.B: It rises smoothly through the surface with no sudden change
options.C: It stays zero across the surface and only grows well outside
options.D: It peaks at the centre and decreases steadily outward through R

distractor_misconceptions.B: expects a continuous field with no jump at the surface
distractor_misconceptions.C: thinks the field stays zero across and beyond the surface
distractor_misconceptions.D: places the maximum at the centre instead of at the surface

questions.tested_idea: E is discontinuous at r=R: 0 just inside, peak kq/R² just outside — a step.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Sketch how the field of a charged Van de Graaff dome changes as a probe is moved from inside the dome to just outside its metal surface.

questions.stem: A thin shell of radius R carries charge q. Using Gauss's law, find the field at r = R/2 and at r = 2R.

options.A: E(R/2) = 0 and E(2R) = kq/(4R²), since inside encloses nothing and outside encloses all of q
options.B: E(R/2) = kq/(R/2)² and E(2R) = kq/(4R²), since the field exists everywhere
options.C: E(R/2) = 0 and E(2R) = kq/R², since outside holds the surface value
options.D: E(R/2) = kq/R² and E(2R) = 0, since the inside is stronger than the outside

distractor_misconceptions.B: applies the outside formula to an interior point that encloses no charge
distractor_misconceptions.C: freezes the external field at the surface value instead of using 1/r²
distractor_misconceptions.D: inverts the regimes, putting the strong field inside and zero outside

questions.tested_idea: q_enc steps from 0 (r<R) to q (r≥R), so E(R/2)=0 and E(2R)=kq/(2R)²=kq/4R².
questions.parallel_form_stem: For a charged dome of radius R, compute the field a charge would feel at half the dome's radius and at twice the dome's radius.

STATE_1.label: We know Φ = q/ε₀ — but what is E(r) inside and outside?

STATE_2.label: By symmetry, E is radial and the same on the whole sphere
STATE_2.formula_overlay: E ∥ r̂,  E = const on the sphere

STATE_3.label: Outside (r ≥ R): Φ = E·4πr² = q/ε₀ ⇒ E = kq/r²

STATE_4.label: From outside, the shell is exactly a point charge at the centre

STATE_6.label: The full picture: flat zero, a jump at R, then 1/r²
STATE_6.formula_overlay: E = 0 (r<R);  jump to kq/R² at R;  E = kq/r² (r≥R)

STATE_7.label: Explore: grab the field point and move it anywhere
STATE_7.formula_overlay: live E: radial, 0 inside, kq/r² outside (same all around r)
